channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India EV Sales Surge on Fuel Price Fears, Challenges Persist
item.description: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia have amplified Indian consumer concerns over fuel price volatility, driving a significant surge in electric vehicle (EV) demand. March EV car sales climbed 49% year-on-year, with industry leaders like Tata Motors, JSW MG Motor, and Mahindra reporting robust growth and increased EV penetration. Despite this momentum, persistent challenges in charging infrastructure, affordability, and evolving government incentives may temper long-term mass adoption.

item.title: India Telcos Called to Boost R&D Amid Supply Chain Costs
item.description: India's Minister of State for Communications has called for increased research and development spending from telecom operators, emphasizing the need to lead in next-generation mobile networks like 6G. Despite sector revenues exceeding ₹3.72 lakh crore in fiscal year 2024-25, domestic R&D investment remains minimal compared to global equipment manufacturers. However, the industry faces significant operational pressures, including escalating equipment costs due to global supply chain disruptions and geopolitical conflicts, which could impede significant R&D investment.

item.title: Hindustan Zinc Eyes Doubled Output on Silver Gains, Demerger Looms
item.description: Hindustan Zinc plans to double refined metal capacity by 2030 with major investments, buoyed by record profits. However, its growth strategy hinges on volatile silver prices, which significantly boost earnings. Talks of a potential demerger, tied to parent Vedanta's restructuring, add strategic complexity as investors assess growth versus commodity risks and valuations.

item.title: ITC Set to Finalize Rs 3,498 Cr Paper Acquisition Next Week
item.description: ITC is set to finalize its ₹3,498 crore acquisition of Century Pulp and Paper next week. The deal, delayed by lease transfers and regulatory approvals, will increase ITC's paper manufacturing capacity by 50%, bolstering its position as India's largest paper producer. Century Pulp's facilities churn out nearly half a million tonnes annually.

item.title: Noida Airport: Foreign CEO Denied Clearance, CFO Takes Interim Role
item.description: Noida International Airport named CFO Nitu Samra as interim CEO. This follows the security clearance denial for former CEO Christoph Schnellmann due to his non-Indian nationality. Schnellmann will move to executive vice chairman. The change ensures regulatory compliance for the airport's launch.

item.title: India Boosts Tax Scrutiny: Stricter ITR Rules for F&O, Donations
item.description: India's Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) has released tougher Income Tax Return (ITR) forms for 2026-27, boosting compliance through data analysis. New rules demand detailed F&O trading turnover, specific political donation reports, and investment data from presumptive taxpayers. While ITR-1 becomes easier to use for some, the overall direction is toward more automated checks for greater financial honesty.

item.title: US Slaps 123% Solar Duty on India; Diversification Cushions Blow
item.description: US imposes a preliminary 123.04% anti-dumping duty on Indian solar cells and modules, citing critical circumstances and non-cooperation from some firms. This move sharply limits US market access, but Indian makers have diversified exports to Europe and Asia. Industry groups are challenging the findings, calling them flawed.

item.title: RIL's JioStar Hits Record Viewership, Posts Strong Q4 Revenue
item.description: Reliance Industries' JioStar reported strong Q4 FY26 performance, with ₹9,784 crore in revenue and ₹827 crore in EBITDA. The platform set a global record for live streaming with 72.5 million peak concurrent viewers during the T20 Men's Cricket World Cup final. It also maintained solid television viewership and grew digital offerings, while Jio Studios' 'Dhurandhar' films achieved major box office success. Although RIL's overall profits declined year-on-year, its digital and retail businesses continue to drive revenue growth.

item.title: Apple Names John Ternus CEO: Faces AI Race and App Store Scrutiny
item.description: Apple's leadership is changing. Tim Cook will become Executive Chairman on September 1, 2026, with hardware chief John Ternus taking over as CEO. Ternus inherits a $4 trillion company facing growing global pressure over its App Store practices and appearing to lag in AI development. Analysts are cautiously optimistic, expecting a renewed focus on hardware innovation, but future growth depends on managing these market and regulatory issues.

item.title: Lodha Developers Targets 10x Annuity Income to ₹3,000 Cr with Data Centers
item.description: Lodha Developers plans to increase its annuity income tenfold, targeting over ₹3,000 crore within six years. This growth will be fueled by significant investments in data centers, retail, warehousing, and office properties. The company recently reported a 24% rise in net profit for the fiscal year ending March.

item.title: Adani Green Capping Growth Over India Grid Limits
item.description: Adani Green Energy is intentionally slowing its renewable capacity expansion to 4.5-5 GW annually, down from its 7-8 GW potential. The company cites major issues with India's power transmission and grid infrastructure, which prevent power from reaching consumers. This slowdown aims to prevent losses from generating power that can't be used, a problem that cost the company an estimated ₹5 billion last year. The constraints are impacting the wider Indian renewable sector and leading to advice for developers to delay project start-ups.

item.title: MF Bulk Deals: SBI MF Buys ADF Foods, ICICI Pru Boosts Pfizer Stake
item.description: On April 24, institutional investors were active. SBI Funds Management acquired a 2% stake in ADF Foods, and ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund substantially increased its holding in Pfizer Limited. Other notable fund trades occurred in VA Tech Wabag and Orient Electric.

item.title: Reliance Industries Hit by Quarterly Margin Squeeze Despite Record Year
item.description: Reliance Industries concluded FY26 with record ₹11.75 lakh crore revenue and over ₹95,700 crore net profit, driven by retail and telecom growth. However, the January-March quarter saw a net profit dip as elevated material costs and energy shocks, fueled by West Asia tensions, compressed margins. Jio's ARPU climbed to ₹214 with a seven-quarter high in subscriber additions. Despite annual strength, the O2C segment faced margin pressures from rising crude premiums and freight costs. The company's stock has seen recent weakness, underperforming the Nifty 50.

item.title: Macrotech Developers' Profit Rises 9% Despite Global Headwinds
item.description: Macrotech Developers (Lodha) announced a 9% jump in Q4 FY26 net profit to ₹1,008 crore, with total income up 9% to ₹4,840 crore. Full-year net profit climbed 24% to ₹3,431 crore. CEO Abhishek Lodha credited this success to focusing on profitable growth and low debt, highlighting strong demand for premium homes despite global challenges. The company is expanding its presence in key markets like Mumbai, Pune, and Bengaluru.

item.title: Air India Delays Navi Mumbai Airport Shift Due to High Costs
item.description: Air India has officially objected to moving its operations to the new Navi Mumbai International Airport (NMIA). The airline told India's airport regulator that proposed fees are "significantly higher" and that splitting operations too soon would lead to higher costs and less efficiency. Air India's concerns question NMIA's expected passenger numbers and show airline caution amid tough industry conditions. The continued use of Mumbai Airport's Terminal 1 also supports Air India's view.

item.title: Mercedes-Benz India Plans July Price Hike on Currency Weakness
item.description: Mercedes-Benz India is planning its third price hike for July, driven by worsening currency exchange rates. This change now presents a bigger challenge than supply chain issues. Demand for luxury cars remains strong despite inflation, though ongoing price increases are approached with caution.

item.title: AWS Bets $12.7B on India, Igniting AI Race Amidst Tech Rivalry
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has committed $12.7 billion to India by 2030, marking aggressive expansion driven by surging demand for cloud and artificial intelligence. This investment positions India as a key part of AWS's global strategy, leveraging the nation's growing digital economy and talent pool. The move intensifies a competitive landscape, with rivals Microsoft and Google also making substantial multi-billion dollar commitments to capitalize on India's booming AI and cloud infrastructure market.

item.title: Supreme Petrochem Sees Strong Q4 Profit; Stock Dips Amid Skepticism
item.description: Supreme Petrochem reported a robust Q4 with net profit up 57.2% to ₹168 crore, significantly recovering from a sharp Q3 decline. Revenue and EBITDA also climbed, alongside margin expansion to 15.96%. The company proposed a ₹8 dividend. Despite these strong results, the stock closed down 1.84% on April 24, 2026, reflecting investor apprehension about the sustainability of the turnaround amid broader industry challenges and a mixed analyst sentiment.

item.title: India's Milk Surplus Battles Hidden Hunger, Private Dairy Leads
item.description: Despite being the world's largest milk producer, India grapples with widespread 'hidden hunger' due to quality and distribution issues. The organized private dairy sector, handling 60% of processed milk, plays an indispensable role. By investing in advanced processing, cold chains, and fortification, these players are crucial for converting milk surplus into dependable nutrition delivery, simultaneously boosting farmer incomes and public health.

item.title: Market Split: Adani Green Climbs, Infosys & Cyient Face Headwinds
item.description: Q4 FY26 earnings reveal a split market. Adani Green Energy posted strong expansion, but Infosys and Cyient face slower growth forecasts and margin pressures. Financial firms like Shriram Finance reported profit growth, while IndusInd Bank returned to profitability. Hindustan Zinc saw its stock fall despite record operations.

item.title: Himadri Opens India's First Anode Plant, Eyes Global Battery Market
item.description: Himadri Speciality Chemical has opened India's first anode material plant, planning to export 70% of its production to the US and Europe. This step leverages growing global demand for lithium-ion batteries and trade advantages over Chinese competitors. The company also reported strong FY26 financial results, including record EBITDA and PAT, and expanded its speciality carbon black capacity.

item.title: Alphabet's $40B AI Bet on Anthropic: Future Growth or Cash Burn?
item.description: Alphabet Inc. is investing $40 billion in AI company Anthropic, significantly deepening their partnership. This move aims to strengthen Alphabet's position in the fast-growing AI sector, which attracted over $242 billion in startup funding in early 2026. The investment escalates the AI arms race, as rivals like Amazon and Microsoft commit heavily to AI developers. While this funding highlights AI's importance for Alphabet's future growth, it also sparks questions about market leadership and long-term profits in a shifting tech environment.

item.title: SMFG Fuels India Growth With ₹1,075 Crore Investment
item.description: Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group has invested ₹1,075 crore in its Indian arm, SMFG India Credit, through a rights issue. This funding signals a stronger commitment to India and powers the NBFC's next growth phase. Recent performance shows strong gains: Assets Under Management (AUM) grew 21% to ₹64,100 crore, and disbursements rose 29% to ₹39,500 crore, paving the way for greater market reach.

item.title: Lodha Developers: Q4 Record Profit Fuels Data Center & Annuity Growth Drive
item.description: Lodha Developers reported a record ₹1,007.9 crore net profit for Q4 FY26. The company is aggressively expanding into data centers and other annuity income streams to de-risk its business model and ensure long-term value creation. Analysts remain highly optimistic, holding a 'Strong Buy' consensus.

item.title: Bitmine (BMNR) Buys $24M Ether, Becomes Largest Public ETH Holder
item.description: Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) executed a $23.87 million over-the-counter purchase of 10,000 ether (ETH) from the Ethereum Foundation. This move significantly expands BMNR's already substantial ETH holdings, now standing at 4.97 million ETH valued at $12.9 billion, positioning it as the largest public ETH holder. The acquisition highlights BMNR's distinct, aggressive accumulation strategy amidst broader market caution. The Ethereum Foundation will leverage these proceeds for operational funding. BMNR aims to acquire 5% of ETH's total supply, signaling a powerful conviction in the digital asset's long-term value and potentially influencing market dynamics.

item.title: Infosys Stock Plunges on Weak AI Forecast and Lowered Growth Guidance
item.description: Infosys reported a 4QFY26 revenue decline of 1.2% sequentially to $5 billion, falling short of forecasts. The company also issued a cautious FY27 revenue growth outlook of 1.5%-3.5% in constant currency. This forecast, combined with a 33.3% drop in large deal bookings to $3.2 billion, caused the stock to plunge nearly 7% on April 24, 2026, to ₹1,154.60. While facing revenue pressures from AI efficiencies impacting the sector, Infosys's adjusted profit beat expectations at ₹88 billion. The company also secured $15 billion in large deal bookings for the full fiscal year. Analysts generally kept 'Hold' ratings, citing tough market conditions and competition.

item.title: UTI AMC: Profits Dip as Analysts Eye Gains
item.description: UTI Asset Management Company (AMC) reported a tough fourth quarter for fiscal year 2026. Revenue from operations dropped to INR 3.7 billion, with earnings missing targets by 11% due to higher costs. While revenue grew 4% year-over-year, it fell 5% from the previous quarter, and management fee earnings decreased. The industry also faces challenges from rising demand for lower-cost passive funds and new regulatory caps on fees. Despite this, analysts are mostly positive, keeping 'Buy' ratings and setting price targets based on expected growth from company initiatives.

item.title: Cipla's Generic Inhaler Approved by USFDA Amid Muted Growth Outlook
item.description: Cipla has received USFDA approval for its first AB-rated generic albuterol inhaler, which can be substituted at pharmacies in the U.S. While this strengthens its respiratory offerings, the actual market opportunity is smaller than the total market size. Analysts expect only modest earnings growth, as competition and pricing challenges may limit the immediate financial benefit.

item.title: LTIMindtree Q4: Analysts Upbeat, But Stock Plunges
item.description: LTIMindtree posted mixed Q4 FY26 results, with sequential revenue and profit growth slowing and Return on Equity falling year-on-year. While some analysts maintain 'Buy' ratings, a significant market reaction on April 24, 2026, saw the stock drop sharply due to missed estimates and weakness in key segments. The company's valuation and outlook are now under close watch amid changing sector trends.

item.title: Cyient's Buyback Premium Clashes With Earnings Miss
item.description: Cyient has announced a ₹7.2 billion share buyback, offering ₹1,125 per share – a premium to its current trading price. This action follows its 4QFY26 results, which missed revenue and profit targets for its Digital, Engineering, and Technology (DET) segment. While Transportation & Mobility grew, other key segments declined. Motilal Oswal maintains a 'Sell' rating with an ₹830 target, citing execution risks and concerns about margin recovery.

item.title: Delhivery Volumes Surge on Smaller Parcels, Faces Valuation Questions
item.description: Delhivery's express logistics segment reported a 43% year-over-year volume increase in Q3 FY26, driven by a surge in smaller parcels. The average shipment weight dropped about 26% YoY, reflecting e-commerce outsourcing and festive demand. However, this trend introduces operational complexities and raises profitability questions, especially given Delhivery's high valuation compared to rivals in a consolidating market.

item.title: ABSL AMC Profit Dips Amid Yield Pressure Despite Strong AUM Growth
item.description: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC reported Q4 FY26 revenue up 7% year-over-year to INR 4.6 billion, but down 4% from the previous quarter. Profit after tax dropped 18% year-over-year due to investment book adjustments and lower management fee yields. Despite overall assets under management growing 17% to INR 4.74 lakh crore, driven by SIPs and alternative assets, gaining equity market share remains difficult. Analysts hold a BUY rating, but new regulations and ongoing margin pressures may impact future valuations.

item.title: Haryana Boosts Senior Living FAR to Spark Boom, Draws Developers
item.description: Haryana's state cabinet has approved a retirement housing policy amendment, significantly increasing the permissible Floor Area Ratio (FAR) to 3.0 via Transferable Development Rights (TDR). This move directly enhances project economics for developers, particularly in high-cost markets like Gurugram, by enabling greater construction density on existing land parcels. Major real estate players, including DLF and Prestige Estates, are actively pursuing opportunities in this burgeoning segment, driven by India's rapidly aging population and evolving consumer aspirations for community-focused, dignified living. While policy support is a catalyst, sustainable sector growth will depend on developers' ability to deliver integrated lifestyle and care services tailored to diverse senior needs.

item.title: West Asia War Hits India's Economy Beyond Oil: Exports, Data, Remittances Suffer
item.description: India's economy faces growing costs from the West Asia war, reaching far beyond oil. Declining exports, soaring shipping expenses, and threatened data infrastructure are creating lasting challenges. Remittances and key sectors like agriculture and real estate are under pressure, signaling a more persistent cycle of rising prices.

item.title: SEBI Targets Uniform Price Bands for F&O Trading
item.description: India's market regulator, SEBI, is planning to introduce a single set of dynamic price bands for all stocks in the futures and options (F&O) trading segment. This aims to end confusion and price distortions caused by different exchanges applying varied rules based on when stocks are added or removed from F&O. The change seeks to create a more consistent trading environment and build investor trust.

item.title: Mercedes-Benz CLA Electric Sedan Debuts in India with 792km Range, Priced from ₹55 Lakh
item.description: Mercedes-Benz India has launched its new CLA electric sedan, priced between ₹55 lakh and ₹64 lakh. The luxury EV boasts an impressive range of up to 792 km and has already received over 400 bookings. Featuring advanced technology and fast charging, this release heats up India's premium electric vehicle market.

item.title: Mercedes-Benz India: CLA EV is Tech Halo, Not Volume Play
item.description: Mercedes-Benz India is launching its upcoming CLA electric sedan as a technology showcase rather than a high-volume seller. Despite over 400 pre-launch bookings, the premium pricing (₹55-64 lakh) and strategic 'value over volume' approach underscore its role as a flagship. This strategy navigates intense competition from global players and Chinese manufacturers, while also anticipating benefits from the India-EU Free Trade Agreement.

item.title: Apple Names John Ternus CEO as AI Race and India Expansion Accelerate
item.description: Apple transitions leadership as Tim Cook hands the CEO reins to hardware veteran John Ternus. Cook's tenure is lauded for operational excellence, market cap expansion into trillions, and strategic supply chain diversification, notably shifting production to India. Ternus faces the challenge of driving product innovation, particularly in artificial intelligence, where Apple is seen as cautious yet poised to leverage privacy as a differentiator against more aggressive rivals.

item.title: India IPOs Halt Amid Geopolitics, Retail Investors Face Promoter Exit Risk
item.description: Geopolitical tensions have slowed India's IPO market, increasing risks for retail investors. Many have lost money when promoters and early investors exit at high valuations. Regulators are offering flexibility, but this addresses a persistent issue where IPOs often prioritize seller payouts over company growth.

item.title: Sebi Proposes Simpler Rules for Unpaid Client Shares
item.description: India's market regulator, Sebi, plans to simplify how trading and clearing members handle unpaid client securities. This aims to cut operational complexity and boost market efficiency for intermediaries, while maintaining strong investor safeguards.
